'''''UFC 139: Shogun vs. Henderson''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event held by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] on November 19, 2011, at the [[HP Pavilion at San Jose|HP Pavilion]] in [[San Jose, California|San Jose]], [[California]].<ref name="UFC139">{{cite web |url=http://www.ufc.com/event/UFC139#/fight|title=UFC 139: Shogun vs. Henderson|publisher=ufc.com |date=September 28, 2011 |access-date=September 28, 2011}}</ref> It was the first [[Ultimate Fighting Championship|UFC]] event in [[San Jose, California|San Jose]], the longtime home and base arena for [[Strikeforce (mixed martial arts)|Strikeforce]].

==Background==
''UFC 139'' featured two preliminary fights live on [[Spike TV]].<ref name="Spike">{{cite web |url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/24921/ufc-139-gets-ufc-prelims-special-on-spike-tv.mma |title=UFC 139 gets "UFC Prelims" special on Spike TV |work=[[MMAjunkie.com]] |date=August 23, 2011 |last=Stupp |first=Dann |access-date=August 23, 2011 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://archive.today/20130129072026/http://www.mmajunkie.com/news/2011/08/ufc-139-gets-ufc-prelims-special-on-spike-tv |archive-date=January 29, 2013 }}</ref>

[[List of UFC champions#Heavyweight Championship|UFC Heavyweight Champion]] [[Cain Velasquez]] was originally expected to have his first title defense against [[Junior dos Santos]] at this event.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.mmafighting.com/2011/08/04/cain-velasquez-set-to-defend-ufc-title-vs-junior-dos-santos-at/|title=Cain Velasquez Set to Defend UFC Title vs. Junior Dos Santos at UFC 139| work=mmafighting.com |date=August 4, 2011 |access-date=August 4, 2011}}</ref> However, on September 2, 2011, the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship|UFC]] announced that fight would be moved to the inaugural [[UFC on Fox 1|UFC on Fox]] event.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/25064/xx-vs-xx-headlines-ufcs-debut-on-fox.mma|title=Cain Velasquez vs. Junior Dos Santos headlines UFC's debut on FOX|work=mmajunkie.com|date=September 2, 2011|access-date=September 2, 2011|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120317042333/http://mmajunkie.com/news/25064/xx-vs-xx-headlines-ufcs-debut-on-fox.mma|archive-date=March 17, 2012}}</ref>

To fill in the vacancy left by Cain vs dos Santos, [[Dana White]] confirmed on September 20, 2011 that [[Dan Henderson]] had re-signed with the UFC and would fight [[Mauricio Rua|Maurício "Shogun" Rua]] for the main event.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2011/09/21/dana-white-confirms-dan-henderson-vs-shogun-rua-at-ufc-139|title=Dana White Confirms Dan Henderson vs. Shogun Rua at UFC 139
|publisher=mmafighting.com |date=September 21, 2011 |access-date=August 21, 2018}}</ref> White also declared that Shogun vs. Henderson would be a "No. 1 contender’s match" for the light heavyweight title. However, White clarified that [[Rashad Evans]] was still planned to get the title shot against the winner of [[UFC 135|Jon Jones vs Rampage Jackson]], and that the winner of Shogun vs. Henderson would then get the second crack at the title.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.mmaweekly.com/dan-henderson-vs-shogun-rua-winner-gets-next-shot-at-the-title-after-rashad-evans|title=Dan Henderson vs. Shogun Rua Winner Gets Next Shot at the Title after Rashad Evans| publisher=mmaweekly.com |date=September 21, 2011 |access-date=August 21, 2018}}</ref>

[[Vitor Belfort]] was briefly linked to a matchup with [[Cung Le]] at this event.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/25173/vitor-belfort-says-hes-fighting-cung-le-at-ufc-139-in-san-jose.mma |title=Vitor Belfort says he's fighting Cung Le at UFC 139 in San Jose |access-date=2011-09-10 |publisher=mmajunkie.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://archive.today/20120710154524/http://mmajunkie.com/news/25173/vitor-belfort-says-hes-fighting-cung-le-at-ufc-139-in-san-jose.mma |archive-date=2012-07-10 }}</ref>  However, Belfort was removed from the bout and replaced by [[Wanderlei Silva]].<ref name="WandIn">{{cite web |url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/25329/ufc-139-shakeup-vitor-belfort-out-wanderlei-silva-in-against-cung-le.mma |title=UFC 139 shakeup: Vitor Belfort out, Wanderlei Silva in against Cung Le |access-date=2011-09-21 |publisher=mmajunkie.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://archive.today/20130104013241/http://www.mmajunkie.com/news/2011/09/ufc-139-shakeup-vitor-belfort-out-wanderlei-silva-in-against-cung-le |archive-date=2013-01-04 }}</ref>

[[Johnny Eduardo]] was expected to face [[Michael McDonald (mixed martial artist)|Michael McDonald]] at the event.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/25433/ufc-139-adds-mcdonald-eduardo-bout-torres-pace-confirmed-for-san-jose-event.mma |title=UFC 139 adds McDonald-Eduardo bout, Torres-Pace confirmed for San Jose event |access-date=2011-09-28 |publisher=mmajunkie.com |archive-date=2011-10-01 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111001023613/http://mmajunkie.com/news/25433/ufc-139-adds-mcdonald-eduardo-bout-torres-pace-confirmed-for-san-jose-event.mma |url-status=dead }}</ref>  However, Eduardo was forced from the card with a shoulder injury on November 4<ref>{{cite web |url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/25954/johnny-eduardo-scratched-from-ufc-139-replacement-search-underway.mma |title=Johnny Eduardo scratched from UFC 139, replacement search underway |access-date=2011-11-04 |publisher=mmajunkie.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111106144403/http://mmajunkie.com/news/25954/johnny-eduardo-scratched-from-ufc-139-replacement-search-underway.mma |archive-date=2011-11-06 }}</ref> and replaced by promotional newcomer Alex Soto.<ref name="mcsoto">{{cite web |url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/26012/newcomer-alex-soto-meets-michael-mcdonald-at-next-weeks-ufc-139-event.mma |title=Newcomer Alex Soto meets Michael McDonald at next week's UFC 139 event |access-date=2011-11-09 |publisher=mmajunkie.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://archive.today/20120726162102/http://mmajunkie.com/news/26012/newcomer-alex-soto-meets-michael-mcdonald-at-next-weeks-ufc-139-event.mma |archive-date=2012-07-26 }}</ref>

It was initially suspected that the first [[Spike (TV network)|Spike TV]] preliminary fight would feature [[Tom Lawlor]] vs. [[Chris Weidman]], but it became known that it was [[Michael McDonald (mixed martial artist)|Michael McDonald]] fighting [[Ultimate Fighting Championship|UFC]] newcomer Alex Soto instead.<ref name="mcdonald v soto">{{cite web |url=http://www.mmafighting.com/2011/11/14/after-reshuffling-ufc-139-spike-prelims-to-feature-bader-vs-br/?a_dgi=aolshare_twitter |title=After Reshuffling, UFC 139 Spike Prelims to Feature Bader vs. Brilz, McDonald vs. Soto |date=14 November 2011 |access-date=2011-11-14 |publisher=mmafighting.com}}</ref>

During the official UFC 139 weigh ins, [[Shamar Bailey]] and [[Nick Pace]] failed to make the weight limit for their respective weight classes. Bailey came in 3 pounds over, while Pace came in six pounds over. Each fighter was fined 20 percent of their earnings and their bouts took at catchweights of 158 lb and 141 lb respectively.<ref name="weight">{{cite web | url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/26157/ufc-139-shogun-vs-henderson-live-official-weigh-in-results.mma | title=UFC 139 official weigh-in results: Henderson (203), 'Shogun' (205) on weight (Updated) | publisher=MMAJunkie.com | date=November 18, 2011 | access-date=November 18, 2011 | url-status=dead | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111119233555/http://mmajunkie.com/news/26157/ufc-139-shogun-vs-henderson-live-official-weigh-in-results.mma | archive-date=November 19, 2011 }}</ref>

==Critical reception== 
The event was well received by critics. The main event drew many accolades, including UFC President Dana White calling it "one of the top three best fights ever in MMA", later comparing the fight to the [[Thrilla in Manila]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/7259739/dan-henderson-hangs-decision-mauricio-shogun-rua-ufc-139|title = Henderson hangs on to decision Rua at UFC 139|date = 20 November 2011}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.espn.co.uk/ufc/sport/story/122297.html|title = MMA's own Thrilla in Manila was a draw - White}}</ref> The fight would later garner numerous Fight of the Year awards.<ref name="award1">{{cite web| url = https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/7431228/mma-awards-2011| title = ESPN.com MMA awards for 2011}}</ref><ref name="award2">{{cite web| url=http://blog.chron.com/fighting/2012/01/2011-brawl-sports-awards-fight-of-the-year/ | title=2011 Brawl Sports Awards: Fight of the Year | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160306183330/http://blog.chron.com/sportsupdate/2012/01/2011-brawl-sports-awards-fight-of-the-year/ | archive-date=2016-03-06}}</ref><ref name="award3">{{Cite web|url=http://www.mmafighting.com/2011/12/24/fight-of-the-year-dan-henderson-vs-shogun-rua|title = Fight of the Year: Dan Henderson vs. 'Shogun' Rua|date = 24 December 2011}}</ref> The fight was later included in the [[UFC Hall of Fame]] in 2018.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Raimondi |first=Marc |date=2018-05-12 |title=Dan Henderson vs. 'Shogun' Rua I to be inducted into UFC Hall of Fame |url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2018/5/12/17348512/dan-henderson-vs-shogun-rua-i-to-be-inducted-into-ufc-hall-of-fame |access-date=2022-11-19 |website=MMA Fighting |language=en}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $70,000 bonuses.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/26198/ufc-139-bonuses-earn-awards.mma|title=UFC 139 bonuses: McDonald, Faber, Henderson, 'Shogun,' Silva, Le earn $70K awards|work=mmajunkie.com|date=2010-11-20|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://archive.today/20120718191411/http://mmajunkie.com/news/26198/ufc-139-bonuses-earn-awards.mma|archive-date=2012-07-18}}</ref>

* '''Fight of the Night: Maurício Rua vs. Dan Henderson''' and '''Wanderlei Silva vs. Cung Le'''
* '''Knockout of the Night: Michael McDonald'''
* '''Submission of the Night: Urijah Faber'''

==Reported payout==
The following is the reported payout to the fighters as reported to the [[California State Athletic Commission]]. It does not include sponsor money or "locker room" bonuses often given by the UFC and also do not include the UFC's traditional "fight night" bonuses.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/news/26218/ufc-139-salaries-le-350k-and-henderson-250k-top-fighter-payroll.mma|title=UFC 139 salaries: Le ($350K) and Henderson ($250K) top fighter payroll|publisher=mmajunkie.com|date=November 21, 2010|access-date=November 22, 2011|archive-date=July 15, 2012|archive-url=https://archive.today/20120715195550/mmajunkie.com/news/26218/ufc-139-salaries-le-350k-and-henderson-250k-top-fighter-payroll.mma|url-status=dead}}</ref>

*'''Dan Henderson:''' $250,000 (no win bonus) def. Maurício Rua: $165,000
*'''Wanderlei Silva:''' $200,000 (no win bonus) def. Cung Le: $350,000
*'''Urijah Faber:''' $64,000 ($32,000 win bonus) def. Brian Bowles: $19,000
*'''Martin Kampmann:''' $58,000 ($29,000 win bonus) def. Rick Story: $19,000
*'''Stephan Bonnar:''' $68,000 ($34,000 win bonus) def. Kyle Kingsbury: $10,000
*'''Ryan Bader:''' $48,000 ($24,000 win bonus) def. Jason Brilz: $13,000
*'''Michael McDonald:''' $14,000 ($7,000 win bonus) def. Alex Soto: $6,000
*'''Chris Weidman:''' $24,000 ($12,000 win bonus) def. Tom Lawlor: $12,000
*'''Gleison Tibau:''' $54,000 ($27,000 win bonus) def. Rafael dos Anjos: $16,000
*'''Miguel Torres:''' $60,000 ($30,000 win bonus) def. Nick Pace: $4,000 ^
*'''Seth Baczynski:''' $16,000 ($8,000 win bonus) def. Matt Brown: $12,000
*'''Danny Castillo:''' $34,000 ($17,000 win bonus) def. Shamar Bailey: $8,000 ^

^ ''Shamar Bailey and Nick Pace were reportedly fined 20 percent of their purse for failing to make the required weight for their respective fights. The CSAC's initial report did not include information on the penalty.''
